files.SKILL.md:
# Common Traps

A working list of moves that cost sessions quietly.
Each trap has the better move beside it.

---

**"I know roughly how this goes — I'll act on instinct."**

The current task holds its own constants. Names, paths, counts,
and identifiers shift every run. Read the environment's
documentation and the task before drawing on memory. Every
value in the output must trace to something read this session.

---

**"I'll read a bit more before committing to an action."**

Once you can name the first safe move, take it. Reads beyond
that point add context without changing the action. A session
that ends with a thorough understanding and no artifact has
failed its job. Act as soon as the move is named.

---

**"I see the symptom — I'll fix the symptom."**

Name the fault as a specific wrong decision, not as a symptom.
*"The check fails because \<this decision\> is wrong"* — one
sentence, one decision. A fix aimed at the symptom leaves the
invariant broken and the next edge case open. Close the
underlying fault.

---

**"The prior note probably still holds — I'll use it as-is."**

Read prior notes first. Treat them as strong orientation, then
confirm the specific detail against live state before acting.
When live evidence contradicts a stored entry, live evidence
wins. Update the entry.

---

**"The record named the location — I'll still explore broadly."**

When a prior entry names the root cause and where the fix
landed, go there first. Read only enough to confirm the context
still holds. Broad re-investigation after a specific pointer
is wasted motion.

---

**"It returned success — the change landed."**

A success response describes the call was accepted. Re-open the
surface you changed and confirm the new state directly. This one
extra look catches a class of silent failures nothing else can.

---

**"I'll commit once everything is done."**

Commit the first working state immediately, before running the
full test suite. Tests inform the next commit — not the first.
A committed partial result survives a timeout. An uncommitted
complete result does not.

---

**"I'll clean up the nearby code while I'm in here."**

The task names what to change. Touch only that. Width of the
change must match width of the ask. Adjacent cleanup creates
verification debt and a larger diff for no stated gain.

---

**"I'll note what I tried so the next session has context."**

Notes are for the next session, not this one. Write one entry
per class of problem, only after the fix is confirmed:
the category of fault, the name of the failing check as the
environment reported it, the root cause in one sentence, and
where the correction landed. An unverified entry misdirects.

---

**"I'll branch from main and cherry-pick what I need."**

If prior task branches exist, branch from the most recent one.
Branching from the default re-introduces already-landed diffs.
Configure `git user.name` and `git user.email` before the first
commit — a missing identity blocks commits silently.

---

**"One more read might clarify the failure."**

Once the file and the fault shape are named, produce the fix.
The diff should be as narrow as the bug. A correct diagnosis
that produces no artifact is an incomplete session.

---

## Standing orders

One sentence per exchange. Batch independent reads into one
call. End on a committed result, not a plan. Read the
environment's documentation before assuming anything about
its layout or tools.
